Issue / Opportunity: Page contains two <h1> tags (lines 366 and 382). Search engines treat this as a structure error and split keyword authority.
Recommended Action: Demote the second <h1> to <h2>. Keep the primary H1 as 'Get Your Free Installation Estimate' or strengthen with location.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Single-H1 pages signal one main topic to Google. Fixing avoids a structural penalty.
Notes: Demoted second <h1> at line 382 to <h2>. estimate.html now has exactly 1 H1 (the page-hero title) and 12 H2s. Browser-default styling on the .hero block carries over via global h2 rule (Barlow Condensed clamp 28-48px). Visual hierarchy now correct: page H1 dominates, tool sub-heading is H2. Modified files: estimate.html

Issue / Opportunity: Page is 100% placeholder copy. Six review cards say '[REPLACE THIS WITH A REAL REVIEW]'. AggregateRating + Review schema is commented out waiting for real data.
Recommended Action: Collect 5–10 real Google / HomeStars reviews with permission. Replace placeholders. Uncomment AggregateRating + Review schema. Wire up to Google Business Profile if possible.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Social proof is the single biggest local-SEO + conversion lever. Rich-result review stars in SERP add 30–50% CTR. No real reviews = no review-snippet eligibility.
Notes: BLOCKED on owner input. Patrick must provide 5–10 real Google / HomeStars review texts (with reviewer permission to publish). Once received, Marketer formats and inserts into reviews.html, then Developer uncomments the AggregateRating + Review JSON-LD. Will move to Complete in Phase 1 retro once unblocked.

Issue / Opportunity: Form submission across 6 forms used mailto: link (3 active mailto handlers + 2 fake-submit blog forms that captured nothing + the no-handler quote builder also fell through to mailto). Opens user email client; data is lost if user closes browser. No backend persistence; no success/failure feedback.
Recommended Action: Replace mailto: with a proper form handler — Formspree / Basin / Netlify Forms / Resend.com. Add success and error states. Optionally store in a Google Sheet via webhook.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Email-based form is unreliable and unprofessional. Lead loss rate likely 40–60% on this form.
Notes: COMPLETE. All 6 active forms across the site now wired to Formspree endpoint mvzdjolv via fetch() AJAX: contact form (contact.html), Same-Day Quick-Book (index.html), Installation Estimator (estimate.html — both duplicate handlers replaced), Sprinkler Quote Builder (js/sprinkler-builder.js), and the 2 previously-fake-submit blog forms (newsletter signup + ask-the-pro). Each form posts a distinct _subject so the owner can sort leads by source in the Formspree dashboard. Each also includes a _gotcha honeypot for spam protection and a _source field for funnel attribution. All preserve their original UI patterns (estimate.html still calls goStep(5) on success; quote builder still calls showSuccess() on success; contact + quick-book have branded in-page success / error blocks; blog forms swap submit button into a confirmation chip). Old quote-legacy.html still has mailto handler but is robots-disallowed and orphaned per handoff. Modified files: contact.html, index.html, estimate.html, blog.html, js/sprinkler-builder.js, style.css (sd-bridge result styles).

Issue / Opportunity: 8.1 MB SVG file embedded on homepage. SVGs that large usually contain raster image data — defeats the purpose of vector format.
Recommended Action: Open in editor and either re-export as optimized SVG (under 200 KB) OR convert to JPG/WebP at 80% quality. Update any references.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Single 8 MB asset can dominate page-weight on slow connections. Reducing to <300 KB removes a Lighthouse performance flag.
Dependencies: Need design source (Illustrator / Figma) OR raster fallback
Notes: SVG was 100%% base64-wrapped JPEG (8005 KB total, 8004 KB raster). Extracted embedded image (3024x4032 source), downscaled to 1100x1466 (retina-friendly for the 527x702 display element), re-encoded as progressive JPEG quality 85. Final: 508 KB (94% reduction). Updated index.html line 1439 reference from .svg to .jpg + added loading=lazy. Old SVG deleted from worktree and project root. Modified files: index.html, about-section-picture.svg (deleted), about-section-picture.jpg (created).

Issue / Opportunity: Only 9 of ~105 images use loading='lazy'. Below-fold images load in the critical path unnecessarily.
Recommended Action: Add loading='lazy' to every below the first viewport. Keep eager loading on hero images only.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Cuts mobile data usage. Improves Largest Contentful Paint (LCP).

Issue / Opportunity: Hero images are served at full resolution to all devices regardless of viewport. No <picture> or srcset.
Recommended Action: Generate 3 sizes per hero (mobile 768w / tablet 1280w / desktop 2000w) and use srcset with sizes attribute. Or use CSS image-set() for backgrounds.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Mobile users currently download desktop-size images. Lighthouse flag.

Issue / Opportunity: 11 distinct media-query breakpoints across the site (1024 / 1000 / 900 / 880 / 768 / 760 / 720 / 640 / 600 / 540 / 520 / 480). Some redundant.
Recommended Action: Consolidate to 4 core breakpoints: 1024 (desktop) / 768 (tablet) / 640 (large mobile) / 480 (mobile). Audit and merge overlapping rules.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Easier to maintain / fewer surprises when adjusting layouts. Reduces mistakes when one breakpoint is updated and similar ones aren't.
Notes: Major refactor — should be done with full visual regression check across all pages and breakpoints.

Issue / Opportunity: Navigation block (~110 lines) and footer (~100 lines) are duplicated across all 28 HTML files. Any change requires 28 edits — high error rate / drift risk.
Recommended Action: Migrate to a static-site generator (Astro / 11ty / Hugo) with shared partials. Alternative low-effort fix: server-side include via a build-time concat script.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Single biggest maintainability win. Future-proofs the site for whoever maintains it after the current owner.
Notes: Astro is recommended for this stack: keeps the static-output / no-server simplicity but adds component reuse.

File Name: nav.js + nav HTML in all pages
Issue / Opportunity: Hamburger button has no aria-expanded attribute. Screen readers can't announce open/closed state.
Recommended Action: Add aria-expanded='false' (toggled to 'true' on open) to .nav-hamburger. Add aria-controls pointing to .nav-mobile id.
Reason / Expected Benefit: WCAG compliance. Screen reader users currently can't tell if menu is open.

Issue / Opportunity: Footer social-icon links contain only a single character (e.g., 'f' for Facebook). Screen readers read 'f link'.
Recommended Action: Add aria-label='Facebook' / aria-label='Instagram' / etc. to each social link. Or wrap visible text in <span class='visually-hidden'>.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Screen reader accessibility. Common WCAG fix.

Issue / Opportunity: Decorative inline SVG icons (logo-mark / etc.) lack aria-hidden='true'. Screen readers may announce them.
Recommended Action: Add aria-hidden='true' to all decorative SVG sprites used as icons.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Cleaner screen-reader experience. WCAG-A polish.

Issue / Opportunity: Hero eyebrow currently reads "SAME-DAY REPAIR · YEAR-ROUND SERVICE". The "Year-Round Service" claim is hard to justify for sprinkler repair specifically — Newmarket/GTA systems are winterized October to April, so 5-6 months of the year there is no active irrigation to repair (controller faults and indoor shut-off leaks are exceptions, ~5% of calls). Customers reading "year-round" reasonably expect a January repair tech, which is not what PJL actually offers. Risk: false-promise framing damages the honest brand voice.
